metadata.title:
SCARCITY OF LABOR IN


ALL PARTS OF COUNTRY

pages.raw_text:
farms in the middle and far west.
To take their places, unskilled Ia
borers have been recruited as farm
hands. A foreign laborer cannot
do the work-and. will not do the
work-of an American farm hand.
For 100000 American farm hands,
it required at least 100000 foreign
ers to care for the American farms.
The majority of these foreigners
were taken from railroad construc-
ton camps, irrigation projects and
building industries where unskilled
labor abounded. The result has
been the labor famine.


The Panama canal too, has
drawn an immense army of unskill-
ed laborers from the United States.
for the government works men only
eight hours a day and pays better
than the railroads.


The panic of 1907 had much to
do with the scarcity of unskilled
labor. Thousands of Italians.
Sicilians, Poles, Hungarians and
Slavs, who do most of the excava-
ton work, returned to their native
homes, for there was no work for
them in America in 1907 and 1908.
